seoTitle: How to Get Your Outlook QR Code for Authenticator
metaDescription: Learn how to generate your Microsoft Outlook security QR code and scan it with an authenticator app to enable multi-factor authentication.
intro: Locking down your Outlook account with an authenticator app adds a strong layer of protection against unauthorized access. This guide walks through finding the security dashboard, generating the QR code, and pairing it with an authenticator app like Microsoft Authenticator on your phone.

steps.heading: Open your security dashboard
steps.body: Navigate to account.microsoft.com and sign into your primary account.

steps.heading: Go to Security settings
steps.body: Click the Security tab in the top application menu.

steps.heading: Open advanced security options
steps.body: Click into the Advanced security options dashboard tile.

steps.heading: Verify your identity
steps.body: Re-enter your credentials or use your device passkey to unlock the advanced protection settings.

steps.heading: Start the QR setup
steps.body: Under Additional security, click Add a new way to sign in or verify, choose Use an app, then select the option to set up a different authenticator app to reveal the QR code.

steps.heading: Scan and confirm
steps.body: Open your authenticator app, tap the plus icon, choose Scan QR code, align your camera with the screen, then enter the rolling 6-digit code to complete the link.

faq.question: What authenticator apps can I use?
faq.answer: Microsoft Authenticator is the native option, but most standard TOTP-based authenticator apps can scan the same QR code.

faq.question: What if my phone camera won't scan the code?
faq.answer: Most authenticator apps also offer a manual entry option using a text code shown alongside the QR image.

faq.question: Should I save backup recovery codes too?
faq.answer: Yes, saving emergency recovery codes is recommended in case you lose access to your authenticator app.

faq.question: How do I know the pairing worked?
faq.answer: Enter the rolling 6-digit numeric code generated by your app into the web validation field to confirm the connection.
